top_comments.text:
That is correct. Some historical context is much appreciated in this thread.

> tl;dr on Section 174, Research & Experimentation costs went from being fully deductible in the year incurred to being deductible over a 5 year period.

Larger tax bills and a tightening on what roles/activities are deductible as R&E are likely what OP is pointing at with his comment.

To the best of my non-inside baseball research, Section 174 changes were simply one part of a package of revenue

Re-reposting this comment https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=28502232 by https://news.ycombinator.com/user?id=heymijo from the 2021 HN discussion https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=28500508 of this Smithsonian article on leaded
I recommend this comment https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=28502232 by https://news.ycombinator.com/user?id=heymijo from the recent HN discussion https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=2850

> Why such a big issue now all of a sudden?

Statcast [0] + Tradition

In the past ten years major technological developments came online. Players for the first time could measure spin rate of baseballs and see how it affected pitches.

Players like Trevor Bauer started experimenting out at Driveline Baseball in Seattle. The innovations diffused and here we are.

Now tradition. Major League pitchers have a long tradition of using substances to help them pitch better. A much to youn

contrarian_analysis.bull_case_flaw: HeyMilo claims to deliver 'fair, consistent evaluations' and 'AI-powered fraud detection', but provides zero transparency on how their models actually work, what data they're trained on, or how bias and false positives are managed. Without published validation or peer-reviewed results, their promise of 'fairness' is marketing, not evidence.
contrarian_analysis.elephant_in_room: How do they ensure their AI doesn't perpetuate bias or make critical mistakes in candidate screening—and who is accountable when it does?

timing_assessment.reasoning: AI-powered recruiting is hot, but the market is flooded with similar offerings and skepticism around automated hiring remains high. Regulatory scrutiny and enterprise trust issues are unresolved; timing could be good if adoption accelerates, but could easily stall if a scandal or regulatory clampdown hits.

incumbent_threat.killer_feature: A native, deeply integrated AI screening/interview module inside Microsoft 365/Teams or Google Workspace, bundled for free or at negligible cost, with enterprise-grade compliance and direct ATS/HRIS integrations.
incumbent_threat.time_to_threat: 3-6 months if prioritized; incumbents already have the distribution, trust, and technical capability.

hidden_assumptions: Enterprises will trust opaque AI systems with hiring decisions.
Regulators will not restrict automated screening in ways that cripple the product.
AI models can reliably detect cheating and fraud without high rates of false positives.
Integration partners (ATS/CRM) won't build competing features themselves.
LLM/API costs will remain low enough to keep margins viable as usage scales.

moat_reality.claimed_moat: Trusted by leading agencies, deep integrations, compliance (GDPR/SOC2), and 'AI Trust Assurance'.
moat_reality.actual_moat: Integrations are table stakes, compliance badges are easy to buy, and trust is unproven without third-party audits or published outcomes. The tech is not differentiated and could be replicated or bundled by larger players.

contrarian_analysis.honest_take: HeyMilo is a feature-rich, well-marketed recruiting AI, but its defensibility and real-world impact are unproven. If the giants get serious, HeyMilo is a rounding error—unless it proves genuine, auditable fairness and accuracy.

primary_trigger.trigger: Recent advances in generative AI (e.g., GPT-4, LLM-powered agents) have enabled highly accurate, context-aware automated candidate screening and interviewing.
primary_trigger.evidence: Widespread enterprise adoption of LLMs for complex workflows, and the launch of specialized APIs for hiring and assessment in 2023-2024.

secondary_triggers.trigger: Enterprise AI budgets surged, with HR tech spend on AI tools growing sharply post-2023.
secondary_triggers.relevance: Companies now have both the budget and mandate to invest in AI-powered hiring solutions.

secondary_triggers.trigger: Growing global regulations (e.g., EU AI Act, NYC Local Law 144) require transparent and auditable AI in hiring.
secondary_triggers.relevance: Organizations need compliant AI screening tools, creating urgency for trustworthy solutions.

secondary_triggers.trigger: Legacy competitors (e.g., HireVue, Modern Hire) rely on outdated models with limited adaptability and transparency.
secondary_triggers.relevance: HeyMilo can differentiate with state-of-the-art generative AI and compliance-first design.

secondary_triggers.trigger: Hybrid and remote work have normalized asynchronous, automated candidate interactions.
secondary_triggers.relevance: Companies and candidates now expect digital-first, AI-driven hiring experiences.

why_now.why_not_earlier: Two years ago, generative AI models were not advanced or reliable enough for nuanced candidate screening, and regulatory clarity around AI in hiring was lacking.
why_now.why_not_later: Delaying would risk losing first-mover advantage as enterprises rapidly adopt new AI hiring platforms and regulatory windows close for non-compliant solutions.
why_now.newsletter_hook: Generative AI breakthroughs and new hiring regulations make now the moment for AI-powered candidate screening platforms like HeyMilo.

takeaways.title: Agentic Orchestration for Multi-Modal Workflows
takeaways.insight: Move beyond rule-based automation by deploying agentic architectures that autonomously route candidates through voice, video, SMS, resume, and form-based screening. This enables context-aware, 24/7 recruiting with minimal human intervention.
takeaways.how_to_apply: Design modular AI agents for each modality (e.g., SMS, voice) and implement an orchestration layer that dynamically assigns candidates to the most suitable channel based on their profile or preference.
takeaways.when_not_to_use: If your use case is single-channel or the candidate pool is small and homogeneous, the complexity of agentic orchestration may not be justified.

takeaways.title: Micro-Model Mesh for Cost-Efficient AI
takeaways.insight: Use specialized micro-models for discrete tasks (e.g., resume parsing, SMS classification) instead of relying solely on large general-purpose LLMs. This reduces inference costs and speeds up processing.
takeaways.how_to_apply: Identify high-frequency, low-complexity tasks and replace LLM calls with lightweight models or rule-based systems. Mesh these micro-models together for end-to-end workflows.
takeaways.when_not_to_use: If your application requires deep semantic understanding or generative capabilities across all steps, micro-models may lack sufficient accuracy.
takeaways.example:
// Use spaCy for resume parsing
import spacy
nlp = spacy.load('en_core_web_sm')
resume_doc = nlp(resume_text)

takeaways.title: Async Multi-Modal Screening
takeaways.insight: Parallelize candidate screening across modalities (voice, SMS, video) using asynchronous processing to minimize latency and maximize throughput.
takeaways.how_to_apply: Implement async job queues for each modality and trigger parallel AI evaluations, aggregating results when all are complete.
takeaways.when_not_to_use: If candidate experience requires synchronous, real-time feedback or if infrastructure doesn't support async processing.
takeaways.example:
async def screen_candidate(candidate):
    results = await asyncio.gather(
        screen_sms(candidate),
        screen_voice(candidate),
        screen_video(candidate)
    )
    return aggregate(results)

takeaways.title: Full Transcript & Score API Access
takeaways.insight: Expose complete screening data (transcripts, scores) via API in JSON format, enabling easy integration and transparency for downstream systems and developers.
takeaways.how_to_apply: Design RESTful API endpoints that return all relevant candidate data in standardized JSON, and document webhook/event triggers for real-time updates.
takeaways.when_not_to_use: If privacy or compliance concerns restrict exposing detailed candidate data, or if downstream systems can't consume JSON.

takeaways.title: Vertical Data Moats for Domain Advantage
takeaways.insight: Build proprietary datasets tailored to your vertical (e.g., recruiting transcripts, fraud patterns) to improve model accuracy and create defensible IP.
takeaways.how_to_apply: Instrument your platform to collect rich, structured data at every candidate touchpoint, and use this data to fine-tune models or power analytics.
takeaways.when_not_to_use: If you lack enough data volume or diversity, or if regulations prohibit storing sensitive information.

quick_wins: Expose full candidate screening data via JSON API endpoints for easy integration.
Replace LLM calls with lightweight micro-models for simple tasks like resume parsing.
Start collecting structured data at every candidate interaction to build your own vertical dataset.
deeper_dives: Research agentic architectures for autonomous workflow orchestration.
Explore async orchestration patterns for multi-modal AI screening.
Investigate vertical data moat strategies and their impact on model performance and defensibility.

headlines.headline: Why HeyMilo is betting AGAINST rule-based recruiting—and what agentic AI means for hiring
headlines.subheadline: HeyMilo's agent-based orchestration challenges the ATS status quo, but can it outpace giants if fairness and accuracy aren't proven?

headlines.headline: I reverse-engineered HeyMilo’s ‘Agentic Recruiting’—here’s what actually runs your interviews
headlines.subheadline: A deep dive into HeyMilo’s autonomous AI agents reveals how far ‘agentic’ really goes beyond workflow bots.

headlines.headline: HeyMilo is using the same agentic playbook as Adept—except for recruiting, not office work
headlines.subheadline: Agentic architectures and vertical data moats: why HeyMilo’s approach echoes the hottest trends in AI ops.

headlines.headline: The ‘Guardrail-as-LLM’ move every recruiting AI team should steal from HeyMilo
headlines.subheadline: HeyMilo bakes guardrails into its agentic system—raising the bar for trust and auditability in automated candidate screening.

headlines.headline: Is HeyMilo just a flashy ATS with agents—or can it actually prove fairness?
headlines.subheadline: Without real-world, auditable impact, HeyMilo risks being a feature, not a platform—unless its agentic claims hold up.

best_headline.headline: I reverse-engineered HeyMilo’s ‘Agentic Recruiting’—here’s what actually runs your interviews
best_headline.reason: This headline promises actionable, insider technical insight for an audience of AI engineers and founders. It teases real analysis of HeyMilo’s core differentiator (agentic recruiting), avoids hype, and positions the reader to learn something they can’t get from a press release.

viral_hooks.social_media_version: I reverse-engineered HeyMilo’s ‘Agentic Recruiting’—here’s what actually runs your interviews

hook.opening_line: What if AI could screen, interview, and evaluate every job candidate—while you sleep?
hook.stakes: The race to automate hiring is on, and whoever cracks it first could reshape how every company finds talent.
hook.why_now: With generative AI maturing and talent wars intensifying, organizations can't afford to miss the next leap in recruiting tech.

discovery.surface_story: HeyMilo positions itself as the all-in-one, AI-powered candidate screening platform for modern organizations.
discovery.deeper_story: Behind the marketing, HeyMilo is quietly building agentic recruiting: autonomous AI agents that orchestrate multi-modal candidate interactions and expose every data point via developer-first APIs.
discovery.surprise: Unlike most HR tech, HeyMilo’s architecture is open and integration-ready—giving customers full access to every screening detail, not just a black-box score.

analysis.technical_insight: HeyMilo leverages agentic architectures and micro-model meshes to route candidates across voice, video, SMS, and more, while continuously learning from every interaction.
analysis.competitive_insight: This developer-centric, transparent approach is rare in recruiting tech, but defensibility is medium—if incumbents or hyperscalers move fast, HeyMilo’s moat could evaporate.
analysis.tension: Bull case: HeyMilo becomes the trusted, auditable backbone for AI hiring. Bear case: It’s just a well-marketed feature set, easily copied by giants with deeper pockets.

resolution.takeaway: The future of recruiting is API-first, agent-driven, and radically transparent—builders should design for integration and data ownership from day one.
resolution.prediction: Expect a wave of vertical AI platforms collecting proprietary datasets and exposing granular APIs, but only those who prove fairness and accuracy will survive the coming scrutiny.
resolution.call_to_action: If you’re building in HR tech or vertical AI, study HeyMilo’s architecture—and start collecting, structuring, and exposing your own data now.

suggested_visuals: Diagram of HeyMilo’s agentic recruiting workflow (multi-modal channels, AI orchestration layer)
Comparison chart: HeyMilo API transparency vs. traditional ATS black-box

unique_voice_content:
```markdown
## HeyMilo: The AI Recruiter That Never Sleeps (But You Probably Should)

Forget the endless parade of “smart” ATS platforms that promise to revolutionize hiring and then leave you stuck with a clunky UI and mystery meat data. HeyMilo wants to automate not just the *process*, but the *judgement*—screening, interviewing, and evaluating candidates via an army of tireless AI agents, in every channel you can think of (voice, video, SMS, resume, forms). It’s the recruiting equivalent of spinning up a fleet of bots to play chess against every applicant, 24/7.

### The Core Insight

What’s actually clever here isn’t just automating tasks or slapping “AI” stickers on old workflows. HeyMilo’s real move is orchestration: agentic architectures that manage multi-modal candidate journeys, with a developer-friendly API that exposes the entire guts—transcripts, scores, real-time events. Think: programmable, extensible recruiting infrastructure, not a black box SaaS. It’s closer to Stripe for hiring data than another “AI-powered ATS.”

### The Technical Meat

Let’s talk architecture. Agentic recruiting is a notch above rule-based automation (if-this-then-that), letting AI agents handle complex, branching workflows across voice, video, and text. Routing candidates by context or preference isn’t trivial—it’s a distributed system problem, and HeyMilo’s orchestration layer seems designed for real extensibility, not just demo-ware.

The data transparency is a breath of fresh air in HR tech, where APIs are usually locked down and “integration” means exporting a CSV. HeyMilo gives you full access (webhooks, transcripts, scoring, all in JSON) and deep integrations with the usual suspects (Greenhouse, Bullhorn, Salesforce, etc.). That’s table stakes for serious enterprise adoption, but it’s rare to see a recruiting platform treat developers as first-class citizens.

But here’s the rub: for all the talk of “fairness” and “cheat detection,” there’s zero visibility into how their models actually work. No published validation, no audits, no clarity on bias or false positives. Integrity detection across voice, video, and text is a genuinely hard problem, especially at scale, and yet we’re asked to take it on faith. The tech stack is a black box—no open source, no custom models, not even a hint about the LLMs powering the magic. For a company selling “AI Trust Assurance,” that’s not reassuring.

### The Honest Assessment

Would I use this? Maybe—as an API playground if I were building something custom and needed multi-modal screening as a service. If I actually cared about fairness, auditability, or regulatory headache, I’d wait for more proof. HeyMilo is built for agencies and enterprises that want to bolt AI onto legacy systems with minimal friction; if you’re a small shop or you want transparency, there are better bets. The differentiation is real, but defensibility is thin—the giants could copy or buy their way in overnight.

### The Builder Takeaway

- **Expose your data:** Transparent APIs and full event access are still rare in vertical SaaS. If you want developers to care, give them real hooks—not just CSV exports.
- **Orchestrate, don’t just automate:** Multi-agent, multi-modal workflows are the future. Building for flexibility (voice, video, text) unlocks new UX, but it’s technically messy. Invest in smart orchestration.
- **Don’t fake “trust”:** If you claim fairness or integrity, publish your validation and let outsiders poke holes. Compliance badges are cheap; real trust takes work.
- **Integrations are necessary, not sufficient:** Deep integrations help win enterprise deals, but they’re not a moat. Assume competitors can match you feature-for-feature.

### The Prediction

In two years, HeyMilo is either a niche power tool for agencies, or it’s been swept aside/absorbed by bigger players who build or buy similar agentic workflows. If they publish real, auditable fairness and accuracy data—and open up their model internals—I’ll happily eat my words.
```
